footer.tagline: A friendlier front door to public benefits. We help you understand what you may qualify for — we never submit anything for you.
footer.finePrint: Aid Compass provides general guidance only. Final eligibility is determined by the agency you apply to. This is not legal or financial advice.
footer.copyright: © {year} Aid Compass. Built for people, not paperwork.
footer.privacyNote: No SSN required · Sessions clear in 2 hours
landing.hero.subtitle: Have a short conversation. We check the real eligibility rules — not a guess — and show which programs fit, what you'd receive, and exactly what to do next.
landing.preview.title: Start wherever you need help
landing.preview.footer: Use one tool or connect all four
landing.how.heading: Three steps from unsure to a plan.
landing.how.step1.title: Tell us a little
landing.how.step1.body: A short, friendly conversation — one question at a time. No name, no SSN, no jargon.
landing.how.step2.title: We check the real rules
landing.how.step2.body: A deterministic engine compares your answers to published 2026 federal and state thresholds.
landing.how.step3.title: You get a clear plan
landing.how.step3.body: See what fits, why, what you'd receive, and exactly which documents to bring.
landing.programs.heading: Programs we check
landing.programs.subtitle: Six federal and state programs across California, Texas, New York, Florida, and Illinois.
landing.trust.heading: Why people trust Aid Compass
landing.trust.rules.title: Rules, not guesses
landing.trust.rules.body: A deterministic engine decides eligibility. The AI never changes the outcome.
landing.trust.sources.title: Sources cited
landing.trust.sources.body: Every result shows its official source, legal basis, and how current the data is.
landing.trust.private.title: Private by design
landing.trust.private.body: No name, no SSN, no immigration status. Your session clears in two hours.
landing.trust.plain.title: Plain language
landing.trust.plain.body: We explain every result in clear, reassuring words — in your chosen language.
landing.trust.free.title: Always free
landing.trust.free.body: Aid Compass costs nothing and never sells you anything. Just guidance.
landing.trust.time.title: A few minutes
landing.trust.time.body: A guided conversation, one question at a time. Most people finish in about three.
landing.faq.heading: Good to know
landing.faq.a1: No. Aid Compass helps you understand what you may qualify for and points you to the official applications. We never submit anything for you.
landing.faq.q2: Do I need to give my Social Security number?
landing.faq.a2: No. The guided check never asks for your name, SSN, or exact address.
landing.faq.q3: Does the AI decide if I qualify?
landing.faq.a3: No. Eligibility comes from a deterministic rules engine using published 2026 program thresholds. The AI only explains the results in plain language.
landing.faq.q4: Which states and programs are covered?
landing.faq.a4: This release covers SNAP, Medicaid, CHIP, LIHEAP, WIC, and TANF across CA, TX, NY, FL, and IL.
landing.faq.q5: Is it available in other languages?
landing.faq.a5: Yes — pick your language from the dropdown in the header. The whole interface and your personalized action plan are translated into the language you choose.
landing.cta.heading: See what you qualify for today.
landing.cta.subtitle: Free, private, and it only takes a few minutes.
program.snap.blurb: Monthly grocery money on a card for low-income households.
program.medicaid.blurb: Free or low-cost comprehensive health coverage for adults.
program.wic.blurb: Food and nutrition support during pregnancy and early childhood.
program.liheap.blurb: Help paying home heating and cooling bills.
program.chip.blurb: Low-cost coverage for children above the Medicaid limit.
program.tanf.blurb: Short-term cash help for families with children.
check.title: Your benefits guide
check.disclaimer: Eligibility is decided by a deterministic rules engine, not by AI. General information only — not legal or financial advice.
check.guide.headingChecking: Checking your answers against the 2026 rules.
check.guide.heading: A few questions, then a real answer.
check.guide.body: We ask one thing at a time, then compare your answers to published federal and state thresholds — no guessing, no judgment.
check.guide.re1: No name, SSN, or immigration status — ever.
check.guide.re2: A deterministic engine decides eligibility, not the AI.
check.guide.re3: Most people finish in about three minutes.
check.checkingCard: Checking your answers against the 2026 rules…
check.msg.startError: I'm having trouble starting right now. Please make sure the server is running, then refresh to try again.
check.msg.resultsReady: Your results are ready — they're on the right. I can answer questions about any of these programs: what they cover, the documents you'll need, or how to apply. What would you like to know?
check.msg.handoffError: I collected your information but couldn't complete the eligibility check. Please try again shortly.
check.msg.intakeError: Sorry, something went wrong. Could you say that again?
check.msg.learnError: Sorry, I couldn't answer that just now. Please try again.
results.heading: Based on what you shared, here's what we found.
results.group.notFit: Probably not a fit right now
results.engineNote: Every result is produced by a deterministic rules engine using published 2026 federal and state thresholds. The AI only puts results into plain language.
plan.modalDesc: Everything we found, in clear words — what fits, why, and exactly what to do next.
plan.next1: Gather the documents listed for each program.
plan.next2: Apply through the official links — one at a time is fine.
plan.next3: The agency reviews your application and makes the final decision.
detail.source: Source: {source}. Data as of {date}. Final eligibility is determined by the agency you apply to.
explain.descSr: Why this eligibility result was produced
explain.footer: Checked deterministically for a household of {count} in {state}. Final eligibility is determined by the agency you apply to.
checklist.heading: Prepare for {program}
checklist.intro: Check these off as you gather them. Nothing is saved to an account — this list lives only in your browser.
checklist.extra1: Write down questions for the agency or caseworker
checklist.extra2: Save or print this action plan for your records
cliff.heading: What happens to your benefits if your income changes?
cliff.intro: Sometimes a raise can briefly leave a household with less overall, because some benefits phase out faster than income rises. That dip is called a benefits cliff.
cliff.howToReadBody: The emerald line combines your monthly income and the estimated value of your benefits. A dip means benefits phase out faster than income rises — so earning a little more can briefly leave you with less.
cloud.notReady: Cloud storage is not ready yet. Check the Supabase integration.
cloud.saveError: Your case file could not be saved. Please try again.
cloud.saved: Your reviewed case file is saved across devices.
cloud.deleteConfirm: Delete the cloud copy? The case file in this browser will remain available.
cloud.deleteError: The cloud copy could not be deleted.
cloud.deleted: Cloud copy deleted. This browser’s local case file is unchanged.
cloud.signedOutBody: Sign in only if you want Compass to store your reviewed case file. Anonymous tools remain fully available.
cloud.storedNote: Only structured results, reviewed details, saved places, and your timeline are stored.
cloud.privacyNote: Original uploaded documents and Google tokens are never stored in Supabase.
ac.heading: Start with whatever feels most urgent.
ac.subtitle: You do not need to complete an eligibility check first. Understand a letter, find local help, or begin a plan now—Compass connects the pieces as you go.
ac.storedNote: Stored only in this browser and cleared with your session.
ac.chooseSubtitle: Each tool works on its own. Using more of them makes your plan more specific.
ac.tool.eligibility.title: Check what you may qualify for
ac.tool.eligibility.body: A short conversation checks published program rules and explains every result.
ac.tool.documents.title: Make sense of a letter or document
ac.tool.documents.body: Upload a notice, paystub, or utility bill. Compass finds dates, useful details, and missing paperwork.
ac.tool.resources.title: Locate help near you
ac.tool.resources.body: Find enrollment offices, clinics, food support, and utility assistance using only a ZIP code.
ac.tool.timeline.title: Turn next steps into a plan
ac.tool.timeline.body: Keep deadlines, documents, appointments, and follow-ups together, then add them to your calendar.
ac.suggest.titleResults: Bring your results into a real plan.
ac.suggest.bodyResults: Your eligibility results are ready. Add a document or nearby location to make the next steps concrete.
ac.suggest.bodyNoResults: If you have a letter with a deadline, begin there. Otherwise, the benefits check takes about three minutes.
ac.label: Action Center
ac.placeholderBody: This tool is the next feature slice being connected.
doc.heading: Turn confusing paperwork into clear next steps.
doc.subtitle: Compass identifies useful details, deadlines, and possible checklist matches. You decide what is correct before anything enters your case file.
doc.privacyBadge: Your file is analyzed once and not stored.
doc.errType: Upload a PDF, JPEG, PNG, or WebP file.
doc.errSize: The file must be smaller than 4 MB.
doc.errAnalyze: This document could not be analyzed.
doc.fileHint: PDF, JPEG, PNG, or WebP · up to 4 MB
doc.control1: Compass shows the exact text behind each extracted detail.
doc.control3: No profile or eligibility result changes automatically.
doc.control4: Agency instructions should still be confirmed with the agency.
doc.saveNote: Saving stores only the structured review in this browser—not the original file.
res.heading: Find the next place that can actually help.
res.subtitle: Search with only a ZIP code. Compass prioritizes relevant places, opening status, and travel time when available.
res.errLoad: Nearby help could not be loaded.
res.emptyTitle: Search by need, not agency name.
res.emptyBody: You can ask for food, health, housing, or a specific program. Compass translates that into nearby service searches.
res.see2: Opening status and travel time when available
res.see3: Direct calling, website, and directions links
res.selectPrompt: Select a place to see its details.
res.mapNotConfigured: Map preview is not configured. Directions and place details still work.
res.detailsSource: Details supplied by {source}. Call ahead to confirm services.
tl.heading: Give every next step a place and a date.
tl.subtitle: Compass combines selected programs, reviewed deadlines, and saved locations. Suggested dates stay visibly different from dates found in your documents.
tl.whenQ: When would you like to submit the first application?
tl.legend.extractedBody: Found in a document you reviewed.
tl.legend.officialBody: Published by a program or agency.
tl.legend.suggestedBody: A planning date you can freely change.
tl.selectedTasksOne: {count} selected task. Review them before creating external events.
tl.selectedTasksOther: {count} selected tasks. Review them before creating external events.
tl.calendarNote: Calendar writes happen only after you select tasks and confirm here.
tl.errNoInput: Choose at least one program or add a reviewed document with a deadline.
tl.errBuild: The timeline could not be built.
tl.gcalConnected: Google Calendar connected. Review the selected tasks before adding them.
tl.gcalNotConnected: Google Calendar was not connected. You can still download the calendar file.
tl.gcalNotConfigured: Google Calendar is not configured on this deployment. Download the calendar file instead.
tl.gcalConnectError: Google Calendar could not be connected.
tl.gcalCreateError: Calendar events could not be created.
